fault_hypothesis: Lines 86-87 contain a spurious post-processing step that removes the last element when both streams end with the same value, incorrectly discarding a valid duplicate observation that should be preserved.

spec.expr: not (merged__1 and left and right and merged__1[-1] == left[-1] == right[-1])

constraints.intent: The condition that triggers the pop should never be true for valid inputs; when it fires it incorrectly removes a duplicate that must be preserved.
After the conditional pop, the merged list length must still equal the sum of both input lengths; the pop violates this invariant.
At the end of each merge loop iteration the merged list length must equal the sum of the two pointers, confirming no elements are dropped inside the loop.
The final merged result must contain exactly as many elements as both inputs combined; the erroneous pop causes this to be one short.
